title: Agent-facing CLI ergonomics: gate verdicts, --json contract, and workflow primitives
description:
Reduce the friction AI agents hit when driving the `jit` CLI, based on mining 42 real Claude Code sessions across this repo and `../gf2`. Ships a stable machine-readable `--json` contract and a small set of gate/issue workflow improvements.

## Background

A multi-agent mining pass over 42 session transcripts surfaced 209 friction findings (full report: `dev/studies/session-mining-jit-improvements.md`). Every proposal was then verified against the newest jit; many were already shipped. The remaining, agreed scope is captured here and decomposed in the planning node's plan doc (`dev/active/53e3fa36-c0cb-4206-8e8e-0a21aafb213e-plan.md`).

Two principles steer the scope: the `--json` surface is changed as a **hard break** (jit has effectively one consumer, so no deprecation shims), and **output volume is the gate checker's responsibility**, not jit's.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] `jit issue show <id> --json` includes `short_id` at root, always renders `labels` and `dependencies` as arrays, and exposes gate state as a single `gates` array `[{key, status, last_run_at, exit_code}]` (no `gates_required` / `gates_status`).
- [ ] `jit issue search --json` returns matches under the `issues` key; the old `results` key is gone.
- [ ] `jit issue show <id> --field state` prints `ready`; `--fields state,title` prints compact JSON; `jit issue show <a> <b> --json` returns a JSON array.
- [ ] `jit gate pass` exits `0` on pass, `1` on checker failure, `2` on runner/infra error; the taxonomy is documented in `--help`; success `--json` carries a `verdict` field.
- [ ] `jit gate pass` on a gate already passed at the current HEAD skips the checker and exits `0`; `--force` re-runs it.
- [ ] `jit gate pass-all <id>` runs the required gates fail-fast, exiting non-zero at the first failure.
- [ ] `jit claim release <issue-id>` releases that issue's active lease without requiring a lease UUID.
- [ ] `jit issue claim` on a dependency-blocked issue prints a message naming `jit issue assign`, and `--assign-only` assigns without transitioning state.
- [ ] `jit issue search --label <ns:val>` returns matches with no positional query argument.
- [ ] `jit issue batch-create --from-json <file>` creates all issues and their dependency edges from one file and returns a keyâ†’id map.
- [ ] `jit dependency`, `jit document`, `jit issue list`, and `--add-label` each resolve to their canonical command/flag.
- [ ] All decomposition tasks are `done` with `cargo-ci` and `code-review` gates passing.

## Notes

- Decomposition, the resolved decision log, and the list of dropped/already-shipped proposals live in the planning node's plan doc (linked above). The breakdown node fans the tasks out from there.
- Dropped after review: `gate wait` (pass is synchronous), inline checker output on fail (checker's job), `gate status` / `gate runs` (covered by `issue show` / last run), `validate` terminal-state exclusion (DAG membership is intentional), `graph deps --flat`, and `issue delete --yes` (deletion friction is deliberate).
